# Code Anchors: Nexus Setup CLI

- `libs/portable-tools/src/nexus-cli.ts` — the single portable `nexus` entrypoint: verb dispatch (deploy, workspace init/status/add-repo), the stdin prompter, and the read-only status path over the resolver.
- `libs/portable-tools/src/deploy-components.ts` — the component-deploy primitive: the overwrite-to-match mirror over the managed set, deleting only retired nxs-namespaced files and leaving user-owned files untouched.
- `libs/portable-tools/src/vendor-components.ts` — vendors the live `.claude/` component tree into the distributable and hashes the managed set for the fingerprint pin; defines the managed subtrees.
- `libs/portable-tools/src/workspace-init.ts` — the `workspace init` verb: sibling discovery, human hub/member designation, per-repo deploy, and writing the declaration.
- `libs/portable-tools/src/workspace-add-repo.ts` — the `workspace add-repo` verb: appends one member to an existing workspace from a new sibling checkout.
- `libs/workspace/src/write.ts` — the manifest/pointer writer: renders a candidate, validates it through the resolver's own parser, and writes only on acceptance; `appendMemberToManifest` is the two-file minimal edit.
- `.claude/commands/nxs.setup.md` — the re-scoped setup command: workspace-role detection through the resolver, no placement prompt, and no manifest or pointer writes.
- `libs/portable-tools/src/deploy-components.spec.ts` — mirror idempotency and user-file safety.
- `libs/portable-tools/src/nexus-cli.spec.ts` — verb dispatch and IO behavior.
- `libs/portable-tools/src/vendor-components.spec.ts` — managed-set vendoring and hashing.
- `libs/portable-tools/src/workspace-init.spec.ts` — init discovery, designation, and resolver parity.
- `libs/portable-tools/src/workspace-add-repo.spec.ts` — add-repo two-file mutation and collision rejection.
- `libs/portable-tools/src/workspace-status.spec.ts` — portable status across hub, member, and single-repo.
- `libs/workspace/src/write.spec.ts` — writer validation-before-write and structured append.
